title: GDIT Awarded $320 Million in Consular Services Task Orders by the Department of State
text: Company to continue delivering U.S. visa support services in South America FALLS CHURCH, Va. , July 27, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- General Dynamics Information Technology (GDIT), a business unit of General Dynamics (NYSE:GD), announced today that it was awarded two recompete task orders totaling $320 million by the U.S. Department of State's Bureau of Consular Affairs to support visa applications and processing in South America.

title: 3 Countercyclical, Intrinsically Undervalued Dividend Aristocrats
text: The market can be unpredictable in the short term, similar to an election, but in the long term, it is more reliable and predictable, like a weighing machine. Long-term investing is beneficial as it allows for compounding and avoids rapid market changes; buying quality stocks and holding onto them is one of the surest ways to alpha. The start of a bull market is an excellent time to buy undervalued long-term dividend stocks, as the crowd's attention is often focused on growth and new technological trends.

title: General Dynamics (GD) Q2 Earnings and Revenues Beat Estimates
text: General Dynamics (GD) came out with quarterly earnings of $2.70 per share, beating the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.59 per share. This compares to earnings of $2.75 per share a year ago.

title: Genernal Dynamics stock rises toward 3-month high after earnings beat, amid record backlog and strong cash flow
text: Shares of General Dynamics Corp. GD, +0.17% rallied 1.8% toward a three-month high in premarket trading Wednesday, after the aerospace and defense company beat second-quarter earnings expectations, amid a record backlog and strong cash flow. Net income slipped to $744 million, or $2.70 a share, from $766 million, or $2.75 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: General Dynamics' revenue rises on private jet, weapon demand
text: Defense contractor General Dynamics Corp on Wednesday reported a jump in its second-quarter revenue, helped by robust demand for business jets and military equipment.

title: General Dynamics Reports Second-Quarter 2023 Financial Results
text: Revenue of $10.2 billion, up 10.5% year-over-year, with growth in all four segments Net earnings $744 million, diluted EPS $2.70 Record-high backlog of $91.4 billion, 1.2-to-1 book-to-bill RESTON, Va. , July 26, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- General Dynamics Corporation (NYSE: GD) today reported second-quarter 2023 net earnings of $744 million on revenue of $10.2 billion.

title: Investing In UAP Disclosure, And How To Manage Risk
text: The US government is investigating Unknown Aerial Phenomenon (UAP) and discussing the Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena Disclosure Act, which could become law. The act would allow the government to seize non-human technologies currently being developed by private corporations, potentially impacting investment markets. Whistleblower David Grusch claims the US government possesses spacecraft reverse-engineered with non-human technology, which could significantly benefit companies such as Lockheed, Northrop Grumman, General Dynamics, Raytheon, and SpaceX.

title: General Dynamics Is Poised For Growth And Way Too Cheap
text: General Dynamics is well-positioned for growth due to demand for its defense hardware and expected growth in munitions and supply chain improvements. The company anticipates cash conversion rates above 100% beyond 2023, with free cash flow allocated to dividends and share repurchases. Thanks to its current undervaluation, General Dynamics stock presents a compelling investment opportunity due to its growth potential and commitment to shareholder returns.

title: General Dynamics Is A Must Have On Your Watchlist
text: General Dynamics, a leading defense contractor, is well-positioned to capitalize on increased defense spending by Western countries due to the ongoing Ukraine war. The company has shown steady growth in sales and earnings, and offers a solid 2.4% dividend yield, making it an attractive option for dividend growth investors. Despite these positives, the company's heavy reliance on the U.S. government for sales and intense competition in the defense sector pose significant risks.
